channel.title: Kostas Stathoulopoulos
channel.copyright: This work is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International License.

item.title: NetSci | Measuring research diversity with graph embeddings
item.description: My submission on a research diversity metric that uses node2vec was accepted for poster presentation at the NetSci 2020. Link to poster Link to abstract

item.title: WOOC | Orion: A research measurement and knowledge discovery tool for scholarly publications
item.description: I presented Orion at the 2nd Workshop on Open Citations and Open Scholarly Metadata 2020. Link to video

item.title: Prototype app: ACL conference explorer
item.description: I made this app ahead of the ACL 2020 conference (link to video). It is intended for the visual exploration and discovery of research publications that have been presented at it. Every particle in the scatterplot is an academic publication. The particles are positioned in space based on the semantic similarity of the paper titles; the closer two points are, the more semantically similar their titles. You can hover over the particles to read their titles and you can click them to be redirected to the original source.

item.title: Orion: Web interface demo
item.description: Zac Ioannidis, my collaborator on Orion, recorded a walkthrough of Orion’s frontend for the IC2S2 conference where we are running a tutorial. Watch the video here.

item.title: IC2S2 | Tutorial on transformers, UMAP, Faiss and HDBSCAN
item.description: Tutorial for the IC2S2 on how to encode text data with transformers and use the embeddings in downstream tasks such as clustering with HDBSCAN, dimensionality reduction with UMAP and vector similarity with Faiss. Link to video

item.title: About
item.description: Hi, I am Kostas! I work at the intersection of machine learning, natural language processing and data engineering. I am currently working as an NLP Engineer at Util, mainly focused on information extraction. Before that, I spent a year at Mozilla as a Machine Learning Engineer and Open Science Fellow. I developed Orion, a knowledge discovery and research measurement platform for scientific documents. It’s open source! I’ve also worked at Nesta as a Senior Data Scientist.

item.title: IC2S2 | A large-scale analysis of the misinformation research landscape
item.description: Presentation at the IC2S2. I examined the geography and composition of misinformation research using publications from Microsoft Academic Graph. I collected the data using Orion, a research measurement and knowledge discovery tool I’ve been developing for Mozilla. Link to video

item.title: Prototype app: Misinformation research explorer
item.description: This application is intended for visual exploration and discovery of research publications on misinformation, disinformation and fake news. Every particle in the visualisation is an academic publication. The particles are positioned in space based on the semantic similarity of the paper abstracts; the closer two points are, the more semantically similar their abstracts. You can hover over the particles to read their titles and you can click them to be redirected to the original source.
